candidates.name: Joe Lombardo
candidates.notes: Governor since 2023. Republican in a purple state. Former Clark County (Las Vegas) Sheriff for 8 years. NRA endorsed in 2022 race. Vetoed multiple gun control bills from Democratic legislature. Signed school choice legislation. Opposes sanctuary policies. On abortion: personally pro-life but said he would not sign a ban stricter than NV's existing law (24 weeks). Moderate on some social issues. Vetoed bill banning conversion therapy.

profile.education: University of Nevada, Las Vegas (BS, Civil Engineering; MS, Crisis Management)
profile.background: Career law enforcement officer; served 30 years with Las Vegas Metropolitan Police Department including as sheriff (2015-2023); became 31st governor in 2023.

candidates.name: Catherine Cortez Masto
candidates.notes: Senator since 2017. First Latina senator in US history. Heritage Action 0% current, 2% lifetime — lowest lifetime among all senators studied. NRA F rated. Former NV Attorney General. Pro-choice. Supports gun control. Supports DREAM Act and pathway to citizenship. Granddaughter of Mexican immigrants.

profile.education: University of Nevada, Reno (BS Finance), Gonzaga University School of Law (JD)
profile.background: Former Nevada Attorney General. First Latina elected to U.S. Senate. Former federal prosecutor and criminal law professor. Daughter of a former Clark County Commissioner.
profile.office: 309 Hart Senate Office Building

district_offices.address: 400 South Virginia Street, Suite 902, Courthouse and Federal Building

candidates.name: Jacky Rosen
candidates.notes: Senator since 2019. Reelected 2024. Heritage Action 0% current. NRA F rated. Former computer programmer and synagogue president (Congregation Ner Tamid, Henderson NV). Pro-choice. Supports assault weapons ban and universal background checks. Supports DREAM Act. Votes reliably with Democratic caucus on all social issues.

profile.background: Former computer programmer and software developer. Former synagogue president at Congregation Ner Tamid. Former U.S. House member. Defeated incumbent Dean Heller in 2018.
profile.office: 713 Hart Senate Office Building

district_offices.address: 400 S Virginia St, Suite 738, Bruce Thompson Federal Building
333 Las Vegas Boulevard South, Suite 8203, Lloyd D. George U.S. Courthouse

profile.office: 2370 Rayburn House Office Building
104 Cannon House Office Building
365 Cannon House Office Building
406 Cannon House Office Building

profile.scoring_rationale: Party-default scoring applied for (D). Democrats in state legislatures vote overwhelmingly with their caucus against each of the six measurable RESOLUTE criteria (abortion rights, immigration accommodation, gun-control, LGBT inclusion, opposition to school choice, and rejection of an explicit Christian framing on policy); all six measurable categories set False. Christian-heritage (PCH/CBG) left null because the absence of evidence there would be misleading either direction — a D member could be a devout Christian in private life.
